title: papaver rhoeas var. arvaticum
intro: Papaver rhoeas var. arvaticum, also known as the wild poppy or red poppy, is a variant of the papaver rhoeas plant species that is native to various parts of the world. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to induce sedation and relieve pain, due to its high concentration of alkaloids such as papaverine and codeine. The health benefits of papaver rhoeas var. arvaticum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate insomnia, and provide relief from anxiety and restlessness. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
